command_help.apply:
Usage: python -m spacy apply [OPTIONS] MODEL DATA_PATH OUTPUT_FILE                                 
                                                                                                    
 Apply a trained pipeline to documents to get predictions. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and    
 path to the data, which can be a directory or a file. The data files can be provided in multiple   
 formats:     1. .spacy files     2. .jsonl files with a specified "field" to read the text from.   
 3. Files with any other extension are assumed to be containing        a single document. DOCS:     
 https://spacy.io/api/cli#apply                                                                     
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    model            TEXT  Model name or path [default: None] [required]                        │
│ *    data_path        PATH  Location of the documents to predict on. Can be a single file in     │
│                             .spacy format or a .jsonl file. Files with other extensions are      │
│                             treated as single plain text documents. If a directory is provided   │
│                             it is traversed recursively to grab all files to be processed. The   │
│                             files can be a mixture of .spacy, .jsonl and text files. If .jsonl   │
│                             is provided the specified field is going to be grabbed ("text" by    │
│                             default).                                                            │
│                             [default: None]                                                      │
│                             [required]                                                           │
│ *    output_file      FILE  Path to save the resulting .spacy file [default: None] [required]    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --code        -c       PATH     Path to Python file with additional code (registered functions)  │
│                                 to be imported                                                   │
│                                 [default: None]                                                  │
│ --text-key    -tk      TEXT     Key containing text string for JSONL [default: text]             │
│ --force       -F                Force overwriting the output file                                │
│ --gpu-id      -g       INTEGER  GPU ID or -1 for CPU. [default: -1]                              │
│ --batch-size  -b       INTEGER  Batch size. [default: 1]                                         │
│ --n-process   -n       INTEGER  number of processors to use. [default: 1]                        │
│ --help                          Show this message and exit.                                      │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.assemble:
Usage: python -m spacy assemble [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH OUTPUT_PATH                                  
                                                                                                    
 Assemble a spaCy pipeline from a config file. The config file includes all settings for            
 initializing the pipeline. To override settings in the config, e.g. settings that point to local   
 paths or that you want to experiment with, you can override them as command line options. The      
 --code argument lets you pass in a Python file that can be used to register custom functions that  
 are referenced in the config.                                                                      
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#assemble                                                            
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      PATH  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
│ *    output_path      PATH  Output directory to store assembled pipeline in [default: None]      │
│                             [required]                                                           │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --code     -c          PATH  Path to Python file with additional code (registered functions) to  │
│                              be imported                                                         │
│                              [default: None]                                                     │
│ --verbose  -V,-VV            Display more information for debugging purposes                     │
│ --help                       Show this message and exit.                                         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.benchmark accuracy:
Usage: python -m spacy benchmark accuracy [OPTIONS] MODEL DATA_PATH                                
                                                                                                    
 Evaluate a trained pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and evaluation data in the binary   
 .spacy format. The --gold-preproc option sets up the evaluation examples with gold-standard        
 sentences and tokens for the predictions. Gold preprocessing helps the annotations align to the    
 tokenization, and may result in sequences of more consistent length. However, it may reduce        
 runtime accuracy due to train/test skew. To render a sample of dependency parses in a HTML file,   
 set as output directory as the displacy_path argument.                                             
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#benchmark-accuracy                                                  
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    model          TEXT  Model name or path [default: None] [required]                          │
│ *    data_path      PATH  Location of binary evaluation data in .spacy format [default: None]    │
│                           [required]                                                             │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --output          -o       FILE       Output JSON file for metrics [default: None]               │
│ --code            -c       PATH       Path to Python file with additional code (registered       │
│                                       functions) to be imported                                  │
│                                       [default: None]                                            │
│ --gpu-id          -g       INTEGER    GPU ID or -1 for CPU [default: -1]                         │
│ --gold-preproc    -G                  Use gold preprocessing                                     │
│ --displacy-path   -dp      DIRECTORY  Directory to output rendered parses as HTML                │
│                                       [default: None]                                            │
│ --displacy-limit  -dl      INTEGER    Limit of parses to render as HTML [default: 25]            │
│ --per-component   -P                  Return scores per component, only applicable when an       │
│                                       output JSON file is specified.                             │
│ --spans-key       -sk      TEXT       Spans key to use when evaluating Doc.spans [default: sc]   │
│ --help                                Show this message and exit.                                │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.benchmark speed:
Usage: python -m spacy benchmark speed [OPTIONS] MODEL DATA_PATH                                   
                                                                                                    
 Benchmark a pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and benchmark data in the binary .spacy    
 format.                                                                                            
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    model          TEXT  Model name or path [default: None] [required]                          │
│ *    data_path      PATH  Location of binary evaluation data in .spacy format [default: None]    │
│                           [required]                                                             │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --batch-size  -b      INTEGER RANGE [x>=1]   Override the pipeline batch size [default: None]    │
│ --no-shuffle                                 Do not shuffle benchmark data                       │
│ --gpu-id      -g      INTEGER                GPU ID or -1 for CPU [default: -1]                  │
│ --batches             INTEGER RANGE [x>=30]  Minimum number of batches to benchmark              │
│                                              [default: 50]                                       │
│ --warmup      -w      INTEGER RANGE [x>=0]   Number of iterations over the data for warmup       │
│                                              [default: 3]                                        │
│ --code        -c      PATH                   Path to Python file with additional code            │
│                                              (registered functions) to be imported               │
│                                              [default: None]                                     │
│ --help                                       Show this message and exit.                         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.convert:
Usage: python -m spacy convert [OPTIONS] INPUT_PATH [OUTPUT_DIR]                                   
                                                                                                    
 Convert files into json or DocBin format for training. The resulting .spacy file can be used with  
 the train command and other experiment management functions.                                       
                                                                                                    
 If no output_dir is specified and the output format is JSON, the data is written to stdout, so you 
 can pipe them forward to a JSON file: $ spacy convert some_file.conllu --file-type json >          
 some_file.json                                                                                     
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#convert                                                             
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    input_path      TEXT          Input file or directory [default: None] [required]            │
│      output_dir      [OUTPUT_DIR]  Output directory. '-' for stdout. [default: -]                │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --file-type        -t       [json|spacy]  Type of data to produce [default: spacy]               │
│ --n-sents          -n       INTEGER       Number of sentences per doc (0 to disable)             │
│                                           [default: 1]                                           │
│ --seg-sents        -s                     Segment sentences (for -c ner)                         │
│ --model,--base     -b       TEXT          Trained spaCy pipeline for sentence segmentation to    │
│                                           use as base (for --seg-sents)                          │
│                                           [default: None]                                        │
│ --morphology       -m                     Enable appending morphology to tags                    │
│ --merge-subtokens  -T                     Merge CoNLL-U subtokens                                │
│ --converter        -c       TEXT          Converter: ('conllubio', 'conllu', 'conll', 'ner',     │
│                                           'iob', 'json')                                         │
│                                           [default: auto]                                        │
│ --ner-map          -nm      PATH          NER tag mapping (as JSON-encoded dict of entity types) │
│                                           [default: None]                                        │
│ --lang             -l       TEXT          Language (if tokenizer required) [default: None]       │
│ --concatenate      -C                     Concatenate output to a single file                    │
│ --help                                    Show this message and exit.                            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.debug config:
Usage: python -m spacy debug config [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH                                          
                                                                                                    
 Debug a config file and show validation errors. The command will create all objects in the tree    
 and validate them. Note that some config validation errors are blocking and will prevent the rest  
 of the config from being resolved. This means that you may not see all validation errors at once   
 and some issues are only shown once previous errors have been fixed. Similar as with the 'train'   
 command, you can override settings from the config as command line options. For instance,          
 --training.batch_size 128 overrides the value of "batch_size" in the block "[training]".           
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#debug-config                                                        
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      PATH  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --code-path,--code  -c      PATH  Path to Python file with additional code (registered           │
│                                   functions) to be imported                                      │
│                                   [default: None]                                                │
│ --show-functions    -F            Show an overview of all registered functions used in the       │
│                                   config and where they come from (modules, files etc.)          │
│ --show-variables    -V            Show an overview of all variables referenced in the config and │
│                                   their values. This will also reflect variables overwritten on  │
│                                   the CLI.                                                       │
│ --help                            Show this message and exit.                                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.debug data:
Usage: python -m spacy debug data [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH                                            
                                                                                                    
 Analyze, debug and validate your training and development data. Outputs useful stats, and can help 
 you find problems like invalid entity annotations, cyclic dependencies, low data labels and more.  
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#debug-data                                                          
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      PATH  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --code-path,--code  -c       PATH  Path to Python file with additional code (registered          │
│                                    functions) to be imported                                     │
│                                    [default: None]                                               │
│ --ignore-warnings   -IW            Ignore warnings, only show stats and errors                   │
│ --verbose           -V             Print additional information and explanations                 │
│ --no-format         -NF            Don't pretty-print the results                                │
│ --help                             Show this message and exit.                                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.debug diff-config:
Usage: python -m spacy debug diff-config [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH                                     
                                                                                                    
 Show a diff of a config file with respect to spaCy's defaults or another config file. If           
 additional settings were used in the creation of the config file, then you must supply these as    
 extra parameters to the command when comparing to the default settings. The generated diff can     
 also be used when posting to the discussion forum to provide more information for the maintainers. 
                                                                                                    
 The `optimize`, `gpu`, and `pretraining` options are only relevant when comparing against the      
 default configuration (or specifically when `compare_to` is None).                                 
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#debug-diff                                                          
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      PATH  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --compare-to                 PATH                   Path to a config file to diff against, or    │
│                                                     `None` to compare against default settings   │
│                                                     [default: None]                              │
│ --optimize          -o       [efficiency|accuracy]  Whether the user config was optimized for    │
│                                                     efficiency or accuracy. Only relevant when   │
│                                                     comparing against the default config.        │
│                                                     [default: efficiency]                        │
│ --gpu               -G                              Whether the original config can run on a     │
│                                                     GPU. Only relevant when comparing against    │
│                                                     the default config.                          │
│ --pretraining,--pt                                  Whether to compare on a config with          │
│                                                     pretraining involved. Only relevant when     │
│                                                     comparing against the default config.        │
│ --markdown          -md                             Generate Markdown for GitHub issues          │
│ --help                                              Show this message and exit.                  │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.debug model:
Usage: python -m spacy debug model [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH COMPONENT                                 
                                                                                                    
 Analyze a Thinc model implementation. Includes checks for internal structure and activations       
 during training.                                                                                   
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#debug-model                                                         
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      PATH  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
│ *    component        TEXT  Name of the pipeline component of which the model should be analysed │
│                             [default: None]                                                      │
│                             [required]                                                           │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --layers       -l         TEXT     Comma-separated names of layer IDs to print                   │
│ --dimensions   -DIM                Show dimensions                                               │
│ --parameters   -PAR                Show parameters                                               │
│ --gradients    -GRAD               Show gradients                                                │
│ --attributes   -ATTR               Show attributes                                               │
│ --print-step0  -P0                 Print model before training                                   │
│ --print-step1  -P1                 Print model after initialization                              │
│ --print-step2  -P2                 Print model after training                                    │
│ --print-step3  -P3                 Print final predictions                                       │
│ --gpu-id       -g         INTEGER  GPU ID or -1 for CPU [default: -1]                            │
│ --help                             Show this message and exit.                                   │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.debug profile:
Usage: python -m spacy debug profile [OPTIONS] MODEL [INPUTS]                                      
                                                                                                    
 Profile which functions take the most time in a spaCy pipeline. Input should be formatted as one   
 JSON object per line with a key "text". It can either be provided as a JSONL file, or be read from 
 sys.sytdin. If no input file is specified, the IMDB dataset is loaded via Thinc.                   
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#debug-profile                                                       
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    model       TEXT      Trained pipeline to load [default: None] [required]                   │
│      inputs      [INPUTS]  Location of input file. '-' for stdin. [default: None]                │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --n-texts  -n      INTEGER  Maximum number of texts to use if available [default: 10000]         │
│ --help                      Show this message and exit.                                          │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

command_help.download:
Usage: python -m spacy download [OPTIONS] MODEL                                                    
                                                                                                    
 Download compatible trained pipeline from the default download path using pip. If --direct flag is 
 set, the command expects the full package name with version. For direct downloads, the             
 compatibility check will be skipped. All additional arguments provided to this command will be     
 passed to `pip install` on package installation.                                                   
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#download AVAILABLE PACKAGES: https://spacy.io/models                
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    model      TEXT  Name of pipeline package to download [default: None] [required]            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --direct  -d,-D            Force direct download of name + version                               │
│ --sdist   -S               Download sdist (.tar.gz) archive instead of pre-built binary wheel    │
│ --url     -U         TEXT  Download from given url [default: None]                               │
│ --help                     Show this message and exit.                                           │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

 Evaluate a trained pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and evaluation data in the binary

 runtime accuracy due to train/test skew. To render a sample of dependency parses in a HTML file,

╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

command_help.find-function:
Usage: python -m spacy find-function [OPTIONS] FUNC_NAME                                           
                                                                                                    
 Find the module, path and line number to the file the registered function is defined in, if        
 available.                                                                                         
                                                                                                    
 func_name (str): Name of the registered function. registry_name (Optional[str]): Name of the       
 catalogue registry.                                                                                
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#find-function                                                       
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    func_name      TEXT  Name of the registered function. [default: None] [required]            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --registry  -r      TEXT  Name of the catalogue registry. [default: None]                        │
│ --help                    Show this message and exit.                                            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.find-threshold:
Usage: python -m spacy find-threshold [OPTIONS] MODEL DATA_PATH PIPE_NAME                          
                                       THRESHOLD_KEY SCORES_KEY                                     
                                                                                                    
 Runs prediction trials for a trained model with varying thresholds to maximize the specified       
 metric. The search space for the threshold is traversed linearly from 0 to 1 in `n_trials` steps.  
 Results are displayed in a table on `stdout` (the corresponding API call to                        
 `spacy.cli.find_threshold.find_threshold()` returns all results).                                  
                                                                                                    
 This is applicable only for components whose predictions are influenced by thresholds - e.g.       
 `textcat_multilabel` and `spancat`, but not `textcat`. Note that the full path to the              
 corresponding threshold attribute in the config has to be provided.                                
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#find-threshold                                                      
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    model              TEXT  Model name or path [default: None] [required]                      │
│ *    data_path          PATH  Location of binary evaluation data in .spacy format                │
│                               [default: None]                                                    │
│                               [required]                                                         │
│ *    pipe_name          TEXT  Name of pipe to examine thresholds for [default: None] [required]  │
│ *    threshold_key      TEXT  Key of threshold attribute in component's configuration            │
│                               [default: None]                                                    │
│                               [required]                                                         │
│ *    scores_key         TEXT  Metric to optimize [default: None] [required]                      │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --n_trials      -n          INTEGER  Number of trials to determine optimal thresholds            │
│                                      [default: 11]                                               │
│ --code          -c          PATH     Path to Python file with additional code (registered        │
│                                      functions) to be imported                                   │
│                                      [default: None]                                             │
│ --gpu-id        -g          INTEGER  GPU ID or -1 for CPU [default: -1]                          │
│ --gold-preproc  -G                   Use gold preprocessing                                      │
│ --verbose       -V,-VV               Display more information for debugging purposes             │
│ --help                               Show this message and exit.                                 │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.info:
Usage: python -m spacy info [OPTIONS] [MODEL]                                                      
                                                                                                    
 Print info about spaCy installation. If a pipeline is specified as an argument, print its meta     
 information. Flag --markdown prints details in Markdown for easy copy-pasting to GitHub issues.    
                                                                                                    
 Flag --url prints only the download URL of the most recent compatible version of the pipeline.     
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#info                                                                
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│   model      [MODEL]  Optional loadable spaCy pipeline [default: None]                           │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --markdown  -md              Generate Markdown for GitHub issues                                 │
│ --silent    -s,-S            Don't print anything (just return)                                  │
│ --exclude   -e         TEXT  Comma-separated keys to exclude from the print-out                  │
│                              [default: labels]                                                   │
│ --url       -u               Print the URL to download the most recent compatible version of the │
│                              pipeline                                                            │
│ --help                       Show this message and exit.                                         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.init config:
Usage: python -m spacy init config [OPTIONS] OUTPUT_FILE                                           
                                                                                                    
 Generate a starter config file for training. Based on your requirements specified via the CLI      
 arguments, this command generates a config with the optimal settings for your use case. This       
 includes the choice of architecture, pretrained weights and related hyperparameters.               
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#init-config                                                         
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮

command_help.package:
Usage: python -m spacy package [OPTIONS] INPUT_DIR OUTPUT_DIR                                      
                                                                                                    
 Generate an installable Python package for a pipeline. Includes binary data, meta and required     
 installation files. A new directory will be created in the specified output directory, and the     
 data will be copied over. If --create-meta is set and a meta.json already exists in the output     
 directory, the existing values will be used as the defaults in the command-line prompt. After      
 packaging, "python -m build --sdist" is run in the package directory, which will create a .tar.gz  
 archive that can be installed via "pip install".                                                   
                                                                                                    
 If additional code files are provided (e.g. Python files containing custom registered functions    
 like pipeline components), they are copied into the package and imported in the __init__.py.       
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#package                                                             
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    input_dir       DIRECTORY  Directory with pipeline data [default: None] [required]          │
│ *    output_dir      DIRECTORY  Output parent directory [default: None] [required]               │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --code              -c                            TEXT  Comma-separated paths to Python file     │
│                                                         with additional code (registered         │
│                                                         functions) to be included in the package │
│ --meta-path,--meta  -m                            FILE  Path to meta.json [default: None]        │
│ --create-meta       -C                                  Create meta.json, even if one exists     │
│ --name              -n                            TEXT  Package name to override meta            │
│                                                         [default: None]                          │
│ --version           -v                            TEXT  Package version to override meta         │
│                                                         [default: None]                          │
│ --build             -b                            TEXT  Comma-separated formats to build: sdist  │
│                                                         and/or wheel, or none.                   │
│                                                         [default: sdist]                         │
│ --force             -f,-F                               Force overwriting existing data in       │
│                                                         output directory                         │
│ --require-parent    -R,-R  --no-require-parent          Include the parent package (e.g. spacy)  │
│                                                         in the requirements                      │
│                                                         [default: require-parent]                │
│ --help                                                  Show this message and exit.              │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.pretrain:
Usage: python -m spacy pretrain [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH OUTPUT_DIR                                   
                                                                                                    
 Pre-train the 'token-to-vector' (tok2vec) layer of pipeline components, using an approximate       
 language-modelling objective. Two objective types are available, vector-based and character-based. 
                                                                                                    
 In the vector-based objective, we load word vectors that have been trained using a word2vec-style  
 distributional similarity algorithm, and train a component like a CNN, BiLSTM, etc to predict      
 vectors which match the pretrained ones. The weights are saved to a directory after each epoch.    
 You can then pass a path to one of these pretrained weights files to the 'spacy train' command.    
 This technique may be especially helpful if you have little labelled data. However, it's still     
 quite experimental, so your mileage may vary.                                                      
 To load the weights back in during 'spacy train', you need to ensure all settings are the same     
 between pretraining and training. Ideally, this is done by using the same config file for both     
 commands.                                                                                          
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#pretrain                                                            
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      FILE  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
│ *    output_dir       PATH  Directory to write weights to on each epoch [default: None]          │
│                             [required]                                                           │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --code          -c       PATH     Path to Python file with additional code (registered           │
│                                   functions) to be imported                                      │
│                                   [default: None]                                                │
│ --resume-path   -r       PATH     Path to pretrained weights from which to resume pretraining    │
│                                   [default: None]                                                │
│ --epoch-resume  -er      INTEGER  The epoch to resume counting from when using --resume-path.    │
│                                   Prevents unintended overwriting of existing weight files.      │
│                                   [default: None]                                                │
│ --gpu-id        -g       INTEGER  GPU ID or -1 for CPU [default: -1]                             │
│ --skip-last     -L                Skip saving model-last.bin                                     │
│ --help                            Show this message and exit.                                    │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
command_help.profile:
Usage: python -m spacy profile [OPTIONS] MODEL [INPUTS]                                            
                                                                                                    
 Profile which functions take the most time in a spaCy pipeline. Input should be formatted as one   
 JSON object per line with a key "text". It can either be provided as a JSONL file, or be read from 
 sys.sytdin. If no input file is specified, the IMDB dataset is loaded via Thinc.

╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮

command_help.train:
Usage: python -m spacy train [OPTIONS] CONFIG_PATH                                                 
                                                                                                    
 Train or update a spaCy pipeline. Requires data in spaCy's binary format. To convert data from     
 other formats, use the `spacy convert` command. The config file includes all settings and          
 hyperparameters used during training. To override settings in the config, e.g. settings that point 
 to local paths or that you want to experiment with, you can override them as command line options. 
 For instance, --training.batch_size 128 overrides the value of "batch_size" in the block           
 "[training]". The --code argument lets you pass in a Python file that's imported before training.  
 It can be used to register custom functions and architectures that can then be referenced in the   
 config.                                                                                            
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#train                                                               
                                                                                                    
╭─ Arguments ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ *    config_path      PATH  Path to config file [default: None] [required]                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --output,--output-path  -o          PATH     Output directory to store trained pipeline in       │
│                                              [default: None]                                     │
│ --code                  -c          PATH     Path to Python file with additional code            │
│                                              (registered functions) to be imported               │
│                                              [default: None]                                     │
│ --verbose               -V,-VV               Display more information for debugging purposes     │
│ --gpu-id                -g          INTEGER  GPU ID or -1 for CPU [default: -1]                  │
│ --help                                       Show this message and exit.                         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Usage: python -m spacy benchmark [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                                       
                                                                                                    
 Commands for benchmarking pipelines.

╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ accuracy   Evaluate a trained pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and evaluation data in │
│            the binary .spacy format. The --gold-preproc option sets up the evaluation examples   │
│            with gold-standard sentences and tokens for the predictions. Gold preprocessing helps │
│            the annotations align to the tokenization, and may result in sequences of more        │
│            consistent length. However, it may reduce runtime accuracy due to train/test skew. To │
│            render a sample of dependency parses in a HTML file, set as output directory as the   │
│            displacy_path argument.                                                               │

│            binary .spacy format.                                                                 │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
unknown_subcommand.debug:
Usage: python -m spacy debug [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                                           
                                                                                                    
 Suite of helpful commands for debugging and profiling. Includes commands to check and validate     
 your config files, training and evaluation data, and custom model implementations.                 
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --help          Show this message and exit.                                                      │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ data          Analyze, debug and validate your training and development data. Outputs useful     │
│               stats, and can help you find problems like invalid entity annotations, cyclic      │
│               dependencies, low data labels and more.                                            │
│ profile       Profile which functions take the most time in a spaCy pipeline. Input should be    │
│               formatted as one JSON object per line with a key "text". It can either be provided │
│               as a JSONL file, or be read from sys.sytdin. If no input file is specified, the    │
│               IMDB dataset is loaded via Thinc.                                                  │
│ config        Debug a config file and show validation errors. The command will create all        │
│               objects in the tree and validate them. Note that some config validation errors are │
│               blocking and will prevent the rest of the config from being resolved. This means   │
│               that you may not see all validation errors at once and some issues are only shown  │
│               once previous errors have been fixed. Similar as with the 'train' command, you can │
│               override settings from the config as command line options. For instance,           │
│               --training.batch_size 128 overrides the value of "batch_size" in the block         │
│               "[training]".                                                                      │
│ diff-config   Show a diff of a config file with respect to spaCy's defaults or another config    │
│               file. If additional settings were used in the creation of the config file, then    │
│               you must supply these as extra parameters to the command when comparing to the     │
│               default settings. The generated diff can also be used when posting to the          │
│               discussion forum to provide more information for the maintainers.                  │
│ model         Analyze a Thinc model implementation. Includes checks for internal structure and   │
│               activations during training.                                                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ config        Generate a starter config file for training. Based on your requirements specified  │
│               via the CLI arguments, this command generates a config with the optimal settings   │
│               for your use case. This includes the choice of architecture, pretrained weights    │
│               and related hyperparameters.                                                       │
│ fill-config   Fill partial config file with default values. Will add all missing settings from   │
│               the default config and will create all objects, check the registered functions for │
│               their default values and update the base config. This command can be used with a   │
│               config generated via the training quickstart widget:                               │
│               https://spacy.io/usage/training#quickstart                                         │
│ vectors       Convert word vectors for use with spaCy. Will export an nlp object that you can    │
│               use in the [initialize] block of your config to initialize a model with vectors.   │

Usage: python -m spacy project [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                                         
                                                                                                    
 Command-line interface for spaCy projects and templates. You'd typically start by cloning a        
 project template to a local directory and fetching its assets like datasets etc. See the project's 
 project.yml for the available commands.                                                            
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --help          Show this message and exit.                                                      │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ assets     Fetch project assets like datasets and pretrained weights. Assets are defined in the  │
│            "assets" section of the project.yml. If a checksum is provided in the project.yml,    │
│            the file is only downloaded if no local file with the same checksum exists.           │
│ clone      Clone a project template from a repository. Calls into "git" and will only download   │
│            the files from the given subdirectory. The GitHub repo defaults to the official       │
│            Weasel template repo, but can be customized (including using a private repo).         │
│ document   Auto-generate a README.md for a project. If the content is saved to a file, hidden    │
│            markers are added so you can add custom content before or after the auto-generated    │
│            section and only the auto-generated docs will be replaced when you re-run the         │
│            command.                                                                              │
│ dvc        Auto-generate Data Version Control (DVC) config. A DVC project can only define one    │
│            pipeline, so you need to specify one workflow defined in the project.yml. If no       │
│            workflow is specified, the first defined workflow is used. The DVC config will only   │
│            be updated if the project.yml changed.                                                │
│ run        Run a named command or workflow defined in the project.yml. If a workflow name is     │
│            specified, all commands in the workflow are run, in order. If commands define         │
│            dependencies and/or outputs, they will only be re-run if state has changed.           │
│ pull       Retrieve available precomputed outputs from a remote storage. You can alias remotes   │
│            in your project.yml by mapping them to storage paths. A storage can be anything that  │
│            the smart_open library can upload to, e.g. AWS, Google Cloud Storage, SSH, local      │
│            directories etc.                                                                      │
│ push       Persist outputs to a remote storage. You can alias remotes in your project.yml by     │
│            mapping them to storage paths. A storage can be anything that the smart_open library  │
│            can upload to, e.g. AWS, Google Cloud Storage, SSH, local directories etc.            │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

Usage: python -m spacy benchmark [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                                       
                                                                                                    
 Commands for benchmarking pipelines.

╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ accuracy   Evaluate a trained pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and evaluation data in │
│            the binary .spacy format. The --gold-preproc option sets up the evaluation examples   │
│            with gold-standard sentences and tokens for the predictions. Gold preprocessing helps │
│            the annotations align to the tokenization, and may result in sequences of more        │
│            consistent length. However, it may reduce runtime accuracy due to train/test skew. To │
│            render a sample of dependency parses in a HTML file, set as output directory as the   │
│            displacy_path argument.                                                               │
│ speed      Benchmark a pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and benchmark data in the     │
│            binary .spacy format.                                                                 │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
group_help.debug:
Usage: python -m spacy debug [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                                           
                                                                                                    
 Suite of helpful commands for debugging and profiling. Includes commands to check and validate     
 your config files, training and evaluation data, and custom model implementations.                 
                                                                                                    
                                                                                                    
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --help          Show this message and exit.                                                      │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ data          Analyze, debug and validate your training and development data. Outputs useful     │
│               stats, and can help you find problems like invalid entity annotations, cyclic      │
│               dependencies, low data labels and more.                                            │
│ profile       Profile which functions take the most time in a spaCy pipeline. Input should be    │
│               formatted as one JSON object per line with a key "text". It can either be provided │
│               as a JSONL file, or be read from sys.sytdin. If no input file is specified, the    │
│               IMDB dataset is loaded via Thinc.                                                  │
│ config        Debug a config file and show validation errors. The command will create all        │
│               objects in the tree and validate them. Note that some config validation errors are │
│               blocking and will prevent the rest of the config from being resolved. This means   │
│               that you may not see all validation errors at once and some issues are only shown  │
│               once previous errors have been fixed. Similar as with the 'train' command, you can │
│               override settings from the config as command line options. For instance,           │
│               --training.batch_size 128 overrides the value of "batch_size" in the block         │
│               "[training]".                                                                      │
│ diff-config   Show a diff of a config file with respect to spaCy's defaults or another config    │
│               file. If additional settings were used in the creation of the config file, then    │
│               you must supply these as extra parameters to the command when comparing to the     │
│               default settings. The generated diff can also be used when posting to the          │
│               discussion forum to provide more information for the maintainers.                  │
│ model         Analyze a Thinc model implementation. Includes checks for internal structure and   │
│               activations during training.                                                       │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯

root_help:
Usage: python -m spacy [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...                                                 
                                                                                                    
 spaCy Command-line Interface                                                                       
                                                                                                    
 DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli                                                                     
                                                                                                    
╭─ Options ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ --install-completion          Install completion for the current shell.                          │
│ --show-completion             Show completion for the current shell, to copy it or customize the │
│                               installation.                                                      │
│ --help                        Show this message and exit.                                        │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
╭─ Commands ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
│ download         Download compatible trained pipeline from the default download path using pip.  │
│                  If --direct flag is set, the command expects the full package name with         │
│                  version. For direct downloads, the compatibility check will be skipped. All     │
│                  additional arguments provided to this command will be passed to `pip install`   │
│                  on package installation.                                                        │
│ info             Print info about spaCy installation. If a pipeline is specified as an argument, │
│                  print its meta information. Flag --markdown prints details in Markdown for easy │
│                  copy-pasting to GitHub issues.                                                  │
│ apply            Apply a trained pipeline to documents to get predictions. Expects a loadable    │
│                  spaCy pipeline and path to the data, which can be a directory or a file. The    │
│                  data files can be provided in multiple formats:     1. .spacy files     2.      │
│                  .jsonl files with a specified "field" to read the text from.     3. Files with  │
│                  any other extension are assumed to be containing        a single document.      │
│                  DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#apply                                            │
│ assemble         Assemble a spaCy pipeline from a config file. The config file includes all      │
│                  settings for initializing the pipeline. To override settings in the config,     │
│                  e.g. settings that point to local paths or that you want to experiment with,    │
│                  you can override them as command line options. The --code argument lets you     │
│                  pass in a Python file that can be used to register custom functions that are    │
│                  referenced in the config.                                                       │
│ convert          Convert files into json or DocBin format for training. The resulting .spacy     │
│                  file can be used with the train command and other experiment management         │
│                  functions.                                                                      │
│ evaluate         Evaluate a trained pipeline. Expects a loadable spaCy pipeline and evaluation   │
│                  data in the binary .spacy format. The --gold-preproc option sets up the         │
│                  evaluation examples with gold-standard sentences and tokens for the             │
│                  predictions. Gold preprocessing helps the annotations align to the              │
│                  tokenization, and may result in sequences of more consistent length. However,   │
│                  it may reduce runtime accuracy due to train/test skew. To render a sample of    │
│                  dependency parses in a HTML file, set as output directory as the displacy_path  │
│                  argument.                                                                       │
│ find-function    Find the module, path and line number to the file the registered function is    │
│                  defined in, if available.                                                       │
│ find-threshold   Runs prediction trials for a trained model with varying thresholds to maximize  │
│                  the specified metric. The search space for the threshold is traversed linearly  │
│                  from 0 to 1 in `n_trials` steps. Results are displayed in a table on `stdout`   │
│                  (the corresponding API call to `spacy.cli.find_threshold.find_threshold()`      │
│                  returns all results).                                                           │
│ package          Generate an installable Python package for a pipeline. Includes binary data,    │
│                  meta and required installation files. A new directory will be created in the    │
│                  specified output directory, and the data will be copied over. If --create-meta  │
│                  is set and a meta.json already exists in the output directory, the existing     │
│                  values will be used as the defaults in the command-line prompt. After           │
│                  packaging, "python -m build --sdist" is run in the package directory, which     │
│                  will create a .tar.gz archive that can be installed via "pip install".          │
│ pretrain         Pre-train the 'token-to-vector' (tok2vec) layer of pipeline components, using   │
│                  an approximate language-modelling objective. Two objective types are available, │
│                  vector-based and character-based.                                               │
│ train            Train or update a spaCy pipeline. Requires data in spaCy's binary format. To    │
│                  convert data from other formats, use the `spacy convert` command. The config    │
│                  file includes all settings and hyperparameters used during training. To         │
│                  override settings in the config, e.g. settings that point to local paths or     │
│                  that you want to experiment with, you can override them as command line         │
│                  options. For instance, --training.batch_size 128 overrides the value of         │
│                  "batch_size" in the block "[training]". The --code argument lets you pass in a  │
│                  Python file that's imported before training. It can be used to register custom  │
│                  functions and architectures that can then be referenced in the config.          │
│ validate         Validate the currently installed pipeline packages and spaCy version. Checks if │
│                  the installed packages are compatible and shows upgrade instructions if         │
│                  available. Should be run after `pip install -U spacy`.                          │
│ debug            Suite of helpful commands for debugging and profiling. Includes commands to     │
│                  check and validate your config files, training and evaluation data, and custom  │
│                  model implementations.                                                          │
│ benchmark        Commands for benchmarking pipelines.                                            │
│ init             Commands for initializing configs and pipeline packages.                        │
│ project          Command-line interface for spaCy projects and templates. You'd typically start  │
│                  by cloning a project template to a local directory and fetching its assets like │
│                  datasets etc. See the project's project.yml for the available commands.         │
╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
